name: E2 (scoped): constant-eta preconditioned-sharpness equilibration ladder in batch size
scope_rationale: A1 (committed) showed NO preconditioned sub-edge mode relaxes on the tau_loss timescale in the spike-robust absolute observable at either scale, so the 'slow mode IS spectral re-pinning' fire branch of the full E2 is already foreclosed -- the expensive eta-escalation stability-probe half is therefore deferred (documented, not silently dropped). What remains untested and cheap is the stochastic-edge account itself: does equilibrated S_pre under CONSTANT eta sit at a noise-set sub-edge level that scales with batch size (the b192 drop arm's rho=0.94 monotone rise hinted at it)?
design: m recipe, resume the G3-replayed trunk_s1337, continue CONSTANT eta=1.5e-3 (peak) at bs in {12, 48, 192} for 4000 steps; spec_nodrop (bs48) already exists; add spec_nodrop_b12 and spec_nodrop_b192 (train_spectrum.py). Same preconditioned top-16 Lanczos probe protocol (fp32, micro-12 HVP, fixed probe batches, Ritz gate 0.05) at the standard dS grid.
metric: equilibrated S_pre = median of kept probes over dS in [800, 4000] (past the bs-change transient); compare across bs and to the deterministic edge 38/eta1 = 25333.

verdicts.STOCHASTIC_EDGE: S_pre,eq increases monotonically with bs AND S_pre,eq(b12) < S_pre,eq(b48) < S_pre,eq(b192) spanning a factor > 2: equilibrated curvature is noise-regulated below the deterministic edge -> the OFF_REGIME sub-band reading is explained as stochastic-edge equilibration; loss relaxation plausibly tracks a noise-set effective edge (mechanism partially reopened as a B-scaling statement, NOT a tau-match claim, which A1 already excluded)
verdicts.DECOUPLED_HARDENS: S_pre,eq flat in bs (within 1.5x) or non-monotone: equilibrated curvature is NOT noise-set; the deterministic-AEoS failure does not convert to a stochastic-edge account either -> OFF_REGIME hardens to fully decoupled at both the deterministic and stochastic edges; slow mode remains phenomenological (strengthened)
verdicts.AMBIG: monotone but factor < 2, or dominated by probe volatility: report the B-scaling exponent as measured, no mechanism claim

deferred_explicitly: eta-escalation instability probing (does raising eta past a candidate stochastic threshold destabilize S_pre): deferred because A1 forecloses its headline upside; recorded as the first prereg item if a future round revisits the spectral account
dof_note: zero formula DOF; pure mechanism measurement (G5 safe)
